sections.paragraph: [the actually occurring form is לְבִבֹות], f.pl. a kind of cake made in a frying pan, as if sagahum of Apicius, prob. so called from their hollow form, twisted together (eine Art zufammengerollter Eierkuchen, Blinzen) [This depends on whether לָבַב has any such meaning as to be hollow; Gesenius says, in Thes., “prob. with plenty of fat”], from the root לָבַב, 2Sa 13:6, 2Sa 13:8, 10 2Sa 13:10. Hence the denominative verb לִבֵּב, which see. LXX. κολλυρίδες. Vulg. sorbitiuncula.
